requirements.full_sentence:
Short Title and Commencement
1 These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Regional Rural
Banks - Asset Liability Management) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Regional Rural Banks - Asset Liability Management) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence: 2 These Directions shall become effective from the date of issue.
requirements.object: date of issue

requirements.full_sentence:
Applicability
3 These Directions shall be applicable to Regional Rural Banks (hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank')
requirements.object: Rural Banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank')

requirements.full_sentence:
Definitions
4 In these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires, the terms herein shall
bear the meanings assigned to them below:
(1) 'Defeasance Period' is the time taken to liquidate the investment in
securities on the basis of liquidity in the secondary market.
requirements.object: to them below: (1) 'Defeasance Period' is the time taken to liquidate the investment in securities on the basis of liquidit

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) 'Interest Rate Risk' means risk where changes in market interest rates
might adversely affect a bank's financial condition.
requirements.object: market interest rates might adversely affect a bank's financial condition

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) 'Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR)' shall have the same meaning as defined in
the Reserve Bank of India (Regional Rural Banks - Cash Reserve Ratio
and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: as defined in the Reserve Bank of India (Regional Rural Banks - Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) Directions, 20

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) 'Statutory Liquidity Ratio (SLR)' shall have the same meaning as defined
in Reserve Bank of India (Regional Rural Banks - Cash Reserve Ratio and
Statutory Liquidity Ratio) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: as defined in Reserve Bank of India (Regional Rural Banks - Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
(5) 'Marginal Standing Facility (MSF)' shall mean the facility as mentioned in
RBI press release dated September 30, 2025.
requirements.object: mentioned in RBI press release dated September 30, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
5 All other expressions unless defined herein shall have the same meaning as
have been assigned to them under the BR Act, the RBI Act, rules / regulations
made thereunder, or any statutory modification or re-enactment thereto or as
used in commercial parlance, as the case may be.
requirements.object: as have been assigned to them under the BR Act, the RBI Act, rules / regulations made thereunder, or any statutory modification

requirements.full_sentence:
Responsibilities of the Board
6 The Board shall have the overall responsibility for management of risks and shall
decide the risk management policy and procedures, set prudential limits,
auditing, reporting and review mechanism in respect of liquidity, interest rate, and
forex risks.
requirements.object: for management of risks and shall decide the risk management policy and procedures, set prudential limits, auditing, r

requirements.full_sentence:
Approval of policies, limits, and reviews
7 The Board shall approve the ALM Policy, exercise the requisite oversight on the
implementation of the ALM system and review its functioning periodically.
requirements.object: exercise the requisite oversight on the implementation of the ALM system and review its functioning periodically

requirements.full_sentence:
8 The Board shall approve the internal prudential limits for cumulative mismatches
(running total) across all time buckets for Structural Liquidity Statement.
requirements.object: limits for cumulative mismatches (running total) across all time buckets for Structural Liquidity Statement

requirements.full_sentence:
9 The Board shall approve prudential limits on individual Gaps of Interest Rate
Sensitivity Statement for a bank.
requirements.object: individual Gaps of Interest Rate Sensitivity Statement for a bank

requirements.full_sentence:
Introduction
10 These Directions shall serve as a benchmark for ALM system for those banks
that lack a formal ALM system.
requirements.object: for ALM system for those banks that lack a formal ALM system

conditions:
In case a bank has introduced more sophisticated
systems
requirements.full_sentence:
In case a bank has introduced more sophisticated
systems, it may continue with it, but shall ensure compliance with ALM systems
suggested in the directions.
requirements.object: systems suggested in the directions

requirements.full_sentence:
ALM, among other functions, shall provide a dynamic
framework for measuring, monitoring and managing liquidity, interest rate and
foreign exchange (forex) risks, involving assessment of various types of risks and
altering balance sheet (assets and liabilities) items in a dynamic manner to
manage risks.
requirements.object: for measuring, monitoring and managing liquidity, interest rate and foreign exchange (forex) risks, involving assessment o

requirements.full_sentence:
11 The ALM system shall rest on the following three pillars:
(1) ALM Information Systems comprising of:
requirements.object: three pillars: (1) ALM Information Systems comprising of:

requirements.full_sentence:
ALM Information Systems
12 ALM shall be supported by specific risk policies and procedures and prudential
limits.
requirements.object: risk policies and procedures and prudential limits

requirements.full_sentence:
The framework shall be built on sound methodology, with the availability
of timely, adequate and accurate information being central to the ALM exercise.
requirements.object: methodology, with the availability of timely, adequate and accurate information being central to the ALM exercise

requirements.full_sentence:
ALM Organisation
13 A bank shall constitute an Asset-Liability Management Committee (ALCO),
headed by the CEO or the Secretary, which shall be responsible for ensuring
<image>
adherence to the policies and limits set by the Board as well as for deciding the
business strategy (on both assets and liabilities) in line with the bank's business
and risk management objectives.
requirements.object: Committee (ALCO), headed by the CEO or the Secretary, which shall be responsible for ensuring <image> adheren

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall approve the ALM Policy,
exercise the requisite oversight on the implementation of the ALM system, and
review its functioning periodically.
requirements.object: exercise the requisite oversight on the implementation of the ALM system, and review its functioning periodically

requirements.full_sentence:
14 The ALCO shall be a decision-making unit responsible for balance sheet
planning from risk-return perspective, including the strategic management of
liquidity, interest rate, and forex risks.
requirements.object: responsible for balance sheet planning from risk-return perspective, including the strategic management of liquidity, intere

requirements.full_sentence:
The ALCO's future business strategy
decisions shall be based on a bank's views on current interest rates.
requirements.object: bank's views on current interest rates

requirements.full_sentence:
The ALCO shall develop a view on future
direction of interest rate movements and decide on funding mixes between fixed
and floating rate funds, wholesale and retail deposits, short term and long-term
deposits, etc.
requirements.object: future direction of interest rate movements and decide on funding mixes between fixed and floating rate funds, wholesale and retail

requirements.full_sentence:
15 The size (number of members) of ALCO shall depend on the size of the bank,
level of business and organisational structure.
requirements.object: of the bank, level of business and organisational structure

requirements.full_sentence:
The Head of the
Information Technology Division responsible for building up of MIS, shall be an
invitee.

An RRB shall, at its discretion, have Sub-committees and Support
Groups.
requirements.object: have Sub-committees and Support Groups

requirements.full_sentence:
16 The ALM Support Groups, consisting of operating staff, shall be responsible for
analysing, monitoring and reporting the risk profiles to the ALCO.
requirements.object: monitoring and reporting the risk profiles to the ALCO

requirements.full_sentence:
The staff shall
also prepare forecasts (simulations) showing the effects of various possible
changes in market conditions related to the balance sheet and recommend the
action needed to adhere to bank's internal limits.
requirements.object: showing the effects of various possible changes in market conditions related to the balance sheet and recommend t

requirements.full_sentence:
17 The business and risk management strategy of the Top Management shall
ensure that it operates within the limits / parameters set by the Board.
requirements.object: within the limits / parameters set by the Board

requirements.full_sentence:
The ALCO
shall inter alia consider pricing of both deposits and advances, desired maturity
profile and mix of the incremental assets and liabilities, etc.
requirements.object: of both deposits and advances, desired maturity profile and mix of the incremental assets and liabilities, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
In addition to
monitoring the risk levels of a bank, the ALCO shall also review the results of and
progress in implementation of the decisions made in the previous meetings.
requirements.object: of and progress in implementation of the decisions made in the previous meetings

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
18 The frequency for holding ALCO meetings shall be decided by the bank.
requirements.object: bank

requirements.full_sentence:
19 A bank shall periodically review and keep the Board informed of the status of
implementation of ALM, including ALM system, functioning of ALCO, status of
computerization, training requirements of officers / staff.
requirements.object: the Board informed of the status of implementation of ALM, including ALM system, functioning of ALCO, status of computeri

requirements.full_sentence:
ALM Process
20 The scope of ALM function shall be as follows, with focus on Liquidity and Interest
rate risk:
(1) Liquidity risk management;
requirements.object: focus on Liquidity and Interest rate risk: (1) Liquidity risk management

requirements.full_sentence:
22 A bank shall measure liquidity positions on an ongoing basis and also examine
how liquidity requirements are likely to evolve under different assumptions /
scenarios.
requirements.object: an ongoing basis and also examine how liquidity requirements are likely to evolve under different assumptions / scenari

requirements.full_sentence:
Maturity Ladder-based monitoring
23 The maturity ladder shall be used as a standard tool for measuring the liquidity
profile under the flow approach, at selected maturity buckets.
requirements.object: standard tool for measuring the liquidity profile under the flow approach, at selected maturity buckets

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall,
accordingly, utilise the format of the Structural Liquidity Statement (SLS) under
static scenario without reckoning future business growth as given in Annex-I.
requirements.object: format of the Structural Liquidity Statement (SLS) under static scenario without reckoning future business growth as given

requirements.full_sentence:
24 The Maturity Profile as given in Annex-IV shall be used for assessing the future
cash flows of a bank in different time buckets.
requirements.object: the future cash flows of a bank in different time buckets
(1) 1 to 14 days

requirements.full_sentence:
25 All cash inflows and outflows in the maturity ladder shall be placed according to
the expected timing of cash flows.
requirements.object: the expected timing of cash flows

requirements.full_sentence:
A maturing liability shall be a cash outflow
while a maturing asset shall be a cash inflow.
requirements.object: while a maturing asset shall be a cash inflow

requirements.full_sentence:
While determining the probable
cash inflows / outflows, a bank shall make appropriate assumptions according to
its asset - liability profile.
requirements.object: to its asset - liability profile

requirements.full_sentence:
While determining the tolerance levels, a bank shall take
into account all relevant factors, based on its asset-liability base, nature of
business, future strategy, etc.
requirements.object: relevant factors, based on its asset-liability base, nature of business, future strategy, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
26 Top Management of a bank shall fix tolerance levels for various maturities in the
SLS, depending on its asset-liability profile, extent of stable deposit base, the
nature of cash flows, etc.
requirements.object: various maturities in the SLS, depending on its asset-liability profile, extent of stable deposit base, the nature of cash fl

requirements.full_sentence:
While mismatches upto one year would be relevant since
these provide early warning signals of impending liquidity problems, the main
focus should be on the short-term mismatches, viz., 1-14 days and 15-28 days
time buckets.
requirements.object: mismatches, viz

requirements.full_sentence:
The mismatches (negative gap between cash inflows and outflows)
during the 1-14 days, and 15-28 days time buckets in normal course should not
exceed 20 per cent of the cash outflows in each time bucket.
requirements.object: of the cash outflows in each time bucket

requirements.full_sentence:
28 The investment in SLR securities and other investment shall be shown under
respective residual maturity buckets, corresponding to the residual maturity.
requirements.object: residual maturity buckets, corresponding to the residual maturity

requirements.full_sentence:
29 A bank which maintains trading book and complies with the following
requirements shall show the trading securities under 1-14 days, 15-28 days, and
29-90 days' time buckets, on the basis of the defeasance periods.
requirements.object: under 1-14 days, 15-28 days, and 29-90 days' time buckets, on the basis of the defeasance periods

requirements.full_sentence:
30 The ALCO of the bank shall approve the volume, composition, holding /
defeasance period, cut loss, etc., of the trading book.
requirements.object: holding / defeasance period, cut loss, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
Behavioural Patterns
31 Notwithstanding guidance provided in Annex-IV, a bank which is better equipped
to reasonably estimate the behavioural pattern of various components of assets
and liabilities on the basis of past data / empirical studies shall classify them in
the appropriate time buckets, subject to approval from the ALCO.
requirements.object: appropriate time buckets

requirements.full_sentence:
Statement of Dynamic Liquidity
32 A bank shall estimate its short-term liquidity profile on the basis of business
projections and other commitments for planning purposes as per format provided
in Annex-II for estimating Short-term Dynamic Liquidity over a time horizon
spanning from 1-90 days.
requirements.object: profile on the basis of business projections and other commitments for planning purposes as per format provided in A

requirements.full_sentence:
Liquidity Adjustment Facility (LAF) and Marginal Standing Facility (MSF)
33 A Scheduled RRB shall be eligible to access the Liquidity Adjustment Facility
(LAF) and the Marginal Standing Facility (MSF) subject to compliance with the
following conditions:
(1) Implemented Core Banking Solution (CBS);
requirements.object: the Liquidity Adjustment Facility (LAF) and the Marginal Standing Facility (MSF) subject to compliance with the following condit

requirements.full_sentence:
The eligibility
status of each bank shall be reviewed on an ongoing basis.
requirements.object: ongoing basis

requirements.full_sentence:
36 A bank with AD authorisation shall set gap limits and also adopt Value at Risk
(VaR) approach to measure the risk associated with forex exposures.
requirements.object: also adopt Value at Risk (VaR) approach to measure the risk associated with forex exposures

requirements.full_sentence:
The open
position limits together with the gap limits shall form part of the risk management
approach to forex operations.
requirements.object: risk management approach to forex operations

requirements.full_sentence:
38 An RRB shall initially use the Traditional Gap Analysis (TGA) for measuring the
Interest Rate Risk.
requirements.object: Gap Analysis (TGA) for measuring the Interest Rate Risk

requirements.full_sentence:
Traditional Gap Analysis
39 Gap analysis means measurement of "Gap", i.e., mismatches between rate
sensitive liabilities and rate sensitive assets (including off-balance sheet
positions) through a Gap Report generated by grouping rate sensitive liabilities,
assets and off-balance sheet positions into time bands according to residual
maturity or next repricing period, whichever is earlier.

40 Under TGA, a bank shall ascertain Gap or Mismatch risk by calculating Gaps,
i.e., mismatches between rate sensitive liabilities and rate sensitive assets, over
different time intervals as at a given date, by preparing Interest Rate Sensitivity
Statement provided in Annex - III.
requirements.object: risk by calculating Gaps, i

requirements.full_sentence:
42 After stabilisation of ALM System and as a bank gains experience, it should
prepare to switch over to more sophisticated techniques like Duration Gap
Analysis, Simulation, and Value at Risk for interest rate risk management.
requirements.object: to more sophisticated techniques like Duration Gap Analysis, Simulation, and Value at Risk for interest rate risk management

requirements.full_sentence:
Interest Rate Sensitivity Statement
43 A bank shall report assets, liabilities, and off-balance sheet positions in the
Interest Rate Sensitivity Statement.
requirements.object: off-balance sheet positions in the Interest Rate Sensitivity Statement

requirements.full_sentence:
44 The rate sensitive liabilities, assets, and off-balance sheet positions shall be
grouped into time buckets according to residual maturity or next repricing period,
whichever is earlier.
requirements.object: buckets according to residual maturity or next repricing period, whichever is earlier

requirements.full_sentence:
The Gaps shall be identified in the following time buckets:
(1) Up to 3 months;
requirements.object: following time buckets: (1) Up to 3 months

requirements.full_sentence:
45 The various items of rate sensitive assets, liabilities and off-balance sheet items
shall be classified as explained in Annex-V.
requirements.object: in Annex-V

requirements.full_sentence:
47 A bank shall set prudential limits on individual Gaps with the approval of the
Board.
requirements.object: individual Gaps with the approval of the Board

requirements.full_sentence:
The prudential limits shall be based on the Total Assets, Earning Assets,
or Equity.
requirements.object: Total Assets, Earning Assets, or Equity

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall also work out Earnings at Risk (EaR) in terms of last
year's NIl or Net Interest Margin (NIM) aligned with its views on interest rate
movements.
requirements.object: at Risk (EaR) in terms of last year's NIl or Net Interest Margin (NIM) aligned with its views on interest rate movements

requirements.full_sentence:
Behavioural Patterns
48 Notwithstanding guidance provided in Annex-V, a bank which is better equipped
to reasonably estimate the behavioural pattern of various components of assets
and liabilities on the basis of past data / empirical studies shall classify them in
the appropriate time buckets, subject to approval from the ALCO.
requirements.object: appropriate time buckets

requirements.full_sentence:
Preparation and Review of Statements 49 A bank shall prepare the SLS for capturing the maturity structure of the cash
flows, as on the last reporting Friday of March / June / September / December
and put up to ALCO / Top Management within a month from the close of the last
reporting Friday.
requirements.object: capturing the maturity structure of the cash flows, as on the last reporting Friday of March / June / September / December and put

requirements.full_sentence:
50 The statement of Short-term Dynamic Liquidity as on each reporting Friday shall
be prepared and put up to the ALCO / Top Management within 2 / 3 days from
the close of the reporting Friday.
requirements.object: up to the ALCO / Top Management within 2 / 3 days from the close of the reporting Friday

requirements.full_sentence:
51 For reporting interest rate sensitive assets and liabilities, a bank shall prepare
Interest Rate Sensitivity (IRS) Statement as on the last reporting Friday of March
/ June / September / December and submit to the ALCO / Top Management within
a month from the last reporting Friday.
requirements.object: (IRS) Statement as on the last reporting Friday of March / June / September / December and submit to the ALCO / Top

requirements.full_sentence:
52 Bank shall submit the SLS and IRS to NABARD as per following schedule:
Sr No Returns for the quarter ending Due date for submission
1 March May 31
2 June August 15
3 September November 15
4 December February 15
<image>
requirements.object: IRS to NABARD as per following schedule: Sr No Returns for the quarter ending Due date for submission 1 March May 31 2 June August

requirements.full_sentence:
The Directions, instructions and
guidelines already repealed shall continue to remain repealed.

54 Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall
continue to be governed by the provisions thereof.
requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or
acknowledgments granted under these repealed lists shall be deemed as
governed by these Directions.
requirements.object: by these Directions
affect: (1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder

requirements.full_sentence:
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may
be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been
repealed.
requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

requirements.full_sentence:
Application of other laws not barred
55 The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation
of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time
being in force.
requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force

requirements.full_sentence:
Interpretations
56 For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order
to remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of
these Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary
<image>
clarifications in respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any
provision of these Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.

Outflows Capital, Reserves and Surplus Over 5 years bucket Demand Deposits (Current and Savings Bank Deposits)
Savings Bank and Current Deposits may be classified into volatile and core portions.
requirements.object: and core portions

requirements.full_sentence: This portion may be treated as volatile.
While volatile portion can be placed in the first time buckets, i.e., 1-14 days, the core portion may be placed in over 1- 3 years time buckets.
requirements.object: 1- 3 years time buckets

requirements.full_sentence: However, the bulk deposits and inter-bank deposits shall be shown under respective residual time buckets.
requirements.object: residual time buckets

requirements.full_sentence: Branch Adjustments The net credit balance may be shown in 1-14 days time bucket.
requirements.object: days time bucket

requirements.full_sentence: Items not representing cash payables (i.e., guarantee fee received in advance, etc.) may be placed in over 5 years' time bucket .
requirements.object: 5 years' time bucket

requirements.full_sentence:
Inflows
<image>
Cash 1-14 days time buckets Balances with | RBI / Public Sector Banks for CRR / SLR purpose
While the excess balance over the required CRR / SLR may be shown under 1-14 days time buckets, the Statutory Balances may be distributed amongst various time bands corresponding to the maturity profile of DTL with a time-lag of 28 days.
requirements.object: days time buckets, the Statutory Balances may be distributed amongst various time bands corresponding to the maturity profile of D

requirements.full_sentence:
Current Account
Non-withdrawable portion on account of stipulations of minimum balances may be shown under over 1-3 years time bucket and the remaining balances may be shown under 1-14 days time bucket.
requirements.object: 1-3 years time bucket and the remaining balances may be shown under 1-14 days time bucket

requirements.full_sentence: Investments classified as NPAs should be shown under over 3-5 years time buckets (sub-standard) or over 5 years time buckets (doubtful).
requirements.object: 3-5 years time buckets (sub-standard) or over 5 years time buckets (doubtful)

requirements.full_sentence: An RRB should undertake a study of behavioural and seasonal pattern of availments based on outstandings and the core and volatile portion should be identified.
requirements.object: behavioural and seasonal pattern of availments based on outstandings and the core and volatile portion should be identified

requirements.full_sentence: While the volatile portion could be shown in the near-term maturity time buckets, the core portion may be shown under over 1-3 year time bucket.
requirements.object: 1-3 year time bucket

requirements.full_sentence: Term Loans Interim cash flows (instalments) should be shown under respective maturity time buckets.
requirements.object: maturity time buckets

requirements.full_sentence:
Other Assets Branch Adjustments
The net debit balance may be shown in 1-14 days time bucket.
requirements.object: days time bucket

requirements.full_sentence: Intangible assets and assets not representing cash receivables may be shown in over 5 years time bucket.
requirements.object: 5 years time bucket

requirements.full_sentence: Bank should undertake a study of the behavioural and seasonal pattern of potential availments in the accounts and the amounts so arrived at may be shown under relevant residual maturity time buckets within 12 months.
requirements.object: residual maturity time buckets within 12 months

requirements.full_sentence:
1-14 days time bucket Letters of Credit / Guarantees devolvement (outflow)
Based on past history, these should be distributed across time buckets.
requirements.object: buckets

requirements.full_sentence: which are known to an RRB and any other contingency may be shown under respective maturity buckets.
requirements.object: maturity buckets

requirements.full_sentence: All overdue liabilities should be placed in the 1-14 days time bucket.
requirements.object: 1-14 days time bucket

requirements.full_sentence:
Interest and instalments from advances and investments, which are overdue for less than one month may be placed in over 3-6
months, time bucket.
requirements.object: 3-6 months, time bucket

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, interest and instalments due (before classification as NPAs) may be placed in over 6-12 months
time bucket if the earlier receivables remain uncollected.
requirements.object: 6-12 months time bucket if the earlier receivables remain uncollected

conditions: In case the negative gap exceeds the prudential limit of 20 per cent of outflows
requirements.full_sentence:
Financing of Gap
In case the negative gap exceeds the prudential limit of 20 per cent of outflows, (1-14 and 15-28 days time buckets) an RRB may show
by way of a foot note as to how it proposes to finance the gap to bring the mismatch within the prescribed limits.
requirements.object: a foot note as to how it proposes to finance the gap to bring the mismatch within the prescribed limits

requirements.full_sentence: This should be included in over 3-6 months time bucket.
requirements.object: 3-6 months time bucket

requirements.full_sentence: The non-interest-paying portion may be shown in non- sensitive bucket.
requirements.object: sensitive bucket

requirements.full_sentence: The amounts should be distributed to different time buckets on the basis of remaining term to maturity.
requirements.object: time buckets on the basis of remaining term to maturity

requirements.full_sentence: The amounts should be distributed to different time buckets on the basis of remaining maturity.
requirements.object: time buckets on the basis of remaining maturity

requirements.full_sentence: The amounts should be distributed to the appropriate time bucket that refers to the resetting date.
requirements.object: appropriate time bucket that refers to the resetting date

requirements.full_sentence: The amounts should be distributed to the respective maturity time bucket.
requirements.object: respective maturity time bucket

requirements.full_sentence: Repos / Bills Re-discounted (DUPN), Sensitive reprices only on maturity and should be distributed to the respective maturity buckets.
requirements.object: respective maturity buckets

requirements.full_sentence: Balances with RBI Interest earning portion may be shown in over 3-6 months time bucket.
requirements.object: 3-6 months time bucket

requirements.full_sentence: The amounts should be distributed to the respective maturity buckets.
requirements.object: respective maturity buckets

requirements.full_sentence: may be shown under over 3-6 months time bucket.
requirements.object: 3-6 months time bucket

requirements.full_sentence: Other products (Interest Rate) (i) Other (i) Should be suitably classified as and when introduced.
requirements.object: and when introduced
